title: senna guatemalensis
intro: Senna guatemalensis, also known as Guatemalan senna or Cassia marilandica, is a plant species native to Central America and parts of North America. The medicinal properties of Senna guatemalensis are primarily attributed to its laxative effects, which are due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, specifically sennosides A and B. The health benefits of Senna guatemalensis include its ability to relieve constipation, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Senna guatemalensis is often prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and can be used to support digestive health, although its long-term use should be carefully monitored due to potential side effects.
